commands.review_summary:
The branch adds account-scoped TokenStore save/load/clear/enumeration APIs with file-backed account indexing, TokenStore tests, and multi-account auth storage documentation.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: two file-backed account IDs such as a host/user pair whose raw strings sanitize to the same value will resolve to the same scoped token filename. I did not run Swift tests because this review is read-only and the PR body already reports the focused test command could not run in its sandbox.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor and maintainer follow-up because proof is missing and the scoped token collision is merge-blocking; no automated repair marker is appropriate while real behavior proof is absent.

target.title: feat(auth): add account-scoped token storage
target.body:
## Summary
- adds account-scoped `TokenStore` APIs for OAuth tokens, OAuth client credentials, and PATs while preserving the legacy single-account wrappers
- adds file-backed account discovery via `allAccountIDs()`, including an index so punctuated account IDs round-trip exactly
- adds focused `TokenStoreTests` coverage for scoped isolation, clear-by-account behavior, legacy coexistence, and account ID enumeration
- documents the Phase 1 account-scoped auth storage schema and links the broader multi-account plan

## Test Plan
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test -- --filter TokenStoreTests` (fails in this Linux sandbox because `swift` is not installed: `./Scripts/test.sh: line 13: swift: command not found`)
target.author: felipeorlando
